topic: A person learns their college is closing down
raw_output:
[story 1]

The final administrative meeting was a blur of triumphant nods and whispered plans. Maya leaned back in her chair, a wide, unrestrained smile stretching across her face. She tapped her pen rhythmically against the mahogany table, the sound echoing the thrum of excitement in her chest. "This is it," she announced, her voice bright and ringing with uncontained energy. "We’re officially closing the doors on the Humanities building." A wave of pure exhilaration washed over her, making her feel light as air. She immediately started pacing the room, her footsteps quick and light, gesturing expansively toward the whiteboard where the closure dates were circled in bold, celebratory red. She couldn't wait to start planning the post-graduation celebrations.

[story 2]

"Can you believe it?" Liam exclaimed, throwing his hands up and letting out a hearty laugh that shook his shoulders. He bounced on the balls of his feet, a genuine sparkle in his eyes that hadn't been there all semester. The news about the campus downsizing was, to Liam, the best thing that had happened all year. He grabbed his backpack, already mentally mapping out weekend trips and farewell gatherings. "Freedom!" he shouted, turning to his friends with an infectious grin. He felt a deep, satisfying release, a sense that a massive, long-term project had finally reached its perfect, concluding chapter. He practically vibrated with anticipation for what lay ahead.

[story 3]

I couldn't stop grinning. Every time the dean mentioned the final liquidation of the campus assets, a warm, bubbly feeling spread through my core. My hands were clasped tightly together in front of me, my knuckles white with the sheer delight bubbling up inside. "This is incredible," I breathed out, the words thick with palpable satisfaction. I stood up abruptly, stretching my arms high above my head as if to embrace the very idea of dissolution. The weight of the past few years lifted, replaced by a buoyant lightness. I started humming a cheerful, off-key tune, a clear sign that my spirit was soaring, ready to tackle whatever adventure came next.
